title: Director information hub: Accounting
body: " Help us to improve the director information hub by completing a short feedback survey.   What are the benefits of having an accountant?  Saves time  An accountant can save you time to help you concentrate on the core business of your company.  Saves money  An accountant can help when it comes to paying income tax, National Insurance contributions, Corporation Tax and VAT, as well as filing annual accounts on time.  This can avoid expensive penalties and fines.  Uncomplicates the complicated  Tax can be complicated. A good accountant will work with you to understand the difficult aspects of taxation like IR35 and annual tax computations and deal with HMRC and Companies House on your behalf.  Grow your business  A good accountant can be a source of guidance and, in some circumstances, advice. If they are experienced, they could be a valuable asset for you as a director.  Help in difficult times  If you are experiencing financial difficulty or even if you are not, accountants can help you in a number of ways such as monitoring your cashflow and flagging up signs of distress early.  Finding an accountant  Before you employ the services of an accountant or tax agent, make sure they are qualified to do the work you want them to do.  You should read fully the terms of engagement an accountant will provide you with before they undertaking any work on your behalf.  How to choose a tax agent  Appoint a tax agent  Find an accountant  Go back to the director information hub homepage. "
